conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I have the most accurate information for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children that you are responsible for?** If yes, how many children do you have?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have three children - Alex who is 3, Blake who is 8, and Charlie who is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have three children: Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (2 years old).

Now, could you please tell me: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Now I need to ask: **Do all three of your children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to check a few more details:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are any of your children currently in hospital for more than 12 weeks, or in care for more than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in hospital or in care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question:

**Are any of your children in paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or receiving benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work or receive any benefits in their own right. They're all quite young - just 3, 8, and 2 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's why you qualify:

✓ **Alex (age 3)** - Eligible: Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming for Alex.

✓ **Blake (age 8)** - Eligible: Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming for Blake.

✓ **Charlie (age 2)** - Eligible: Charlie is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming for Charlie.

You meet all the key eligibility requirements: you live in the UK, you're responsible for your children (they live with you), they're all under 16, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit.

You can apply for Child Benefit through the government's application process. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

The claimant meets all eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for all three children.
